rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although any supplement or amendment to a pleading must be signed and verified by the executor, not his attorney. [CRC 7.103(c).] The receipts were not attached at pp. 75-84 to a "First Status Report," which was 56 pages long, they were attached to the amended first account and report of status, etc., filed on 10/19/16. There was no "Second Sussman Decl." It is unclear why the beneficiaries of the pecuniary bequests are still receiving notice since their distributions have been satisfied. It would be helpful in future pleadings if Counsel referred to filing dates and the proper titles of pleadings not to page numbers of pdfs without the filing date. Status of closing the estate is set for 12/9/20, which is far beyond the four months requested in this fifth report filed in February 2020 and set for hearing in April.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOE W. THORN SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/14/20. Appearance will likely be required as no Final I&A has been filed yet, when the Spousal Property Petition was denied in March 2018. No proof of Suzie Thorn's reimbursement to the estate for the $550 cost for the appraisal of 212 Amber Drive, SF (property that reportedly passed to her outside of probate) has been filed yet. Evidence of the other partners' proportionate contributions to SW3 may be required; the partnership agreement alone does not support the amounts disbursed to SW3 as "capital calls". It is unclear why this estate is still paying mortgage, HOA dues, utilities, and management fees for the property in Ashland, OR, (not an asset of this California estate) bequeathed to the Ashland Shakespeare Festival, or why there is no income from the wife, friends, and relatives who are permitted to stay at that property for the first five years for a charge to cover reasonable costs of maintaining that property, as directed in the will.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/14/20. Notice of the first amendment filed on 8/31/20 was mailed on 9/2/20, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. [CRC 7.53(a).] If bond must be increased, an ex parte petition should be filed promptly. [CRC 7.204.] Notice must be given at least 15 days before the continued hearing. A courtesy copy of the proof of service must be delivered promptly to the assigned Examiner and by no later than 10/2/20.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BERNYL L. REED AND ANNA K. REED 1989 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: (1) Breach Of Trust; (2) Compelling Trustee To Return Trust Property To Trust; (3) Compelling Distribution From The Trust; (4) Compellin
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Both counsel must appear to update the Court. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHAWNTE L. PACHECO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/14/20. (No earlier date available.) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. (If the decedent was survived by a domestic partner, that person, who would have priority, must receive notice.) Item 8 of the petition does not indicate who the decedent's mother is/was or when she died, if she is deceased. [LRSF 14.26 D.] Although a petitioner does not need to complete the waiver of bond for himself, the Waiver of Bond attached to the petition is incomplete at item 4. Although a petitioner does not need to give notice to himself, item 4a of the proof of service is incomplete. (Both items 2d(1) and (2) of the petition were checked, which are contradictory requests; however a non-resident personal representative will be required to post at least minimum bond. [LRSF 14.37.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY NGAI YING CHEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/14/20. An amended petition (or an amendment to a petition, which the amended petition filed on 8/24/20 was not) requires the same notice as the pleading it amends, including publication. [CRC 7.53(a).] Notice was mailed on 9/9/20, which was not at least 15 days before 9/16/20. Proof of service and proof of publication of the amended petition (erroneously captioned "Amendment to Petition," etc., must be filed in time to deliver courtesy copies to the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/5/20. (The date of death of any heir should be stated in item 8 of the petition.)
